title: Revelation Begins
description: Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) receives the first revelation in the Cave of Hira on Mount Noor, marking the beginning of his prophethood.

title: The First Muslims
description: Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, Ali ibn Abi Talib, Abu Bakr As-Siddiq, and Zayd ibn Harithah are among the first to embrace Islam.

title: Early Conversions Through Abu Bakr
description: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq brings several people to Islam, including Uthman ibn Affan, Zubayr ibn Al-Awwam, Talha ibn Ubaydullah, Abdur-Rahman ibn Awf, and Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas.

title: Persecution of the Early Muslims
description: The Quraysh intensify persecution of Muslims in Makkah. Many early converts face torture, boycotts, and social isolation.

title: Migration to Abyssinia
description: A group of Muslims migrates to Abyssinia (modern-day Ethiopia) seeking refuge under the just Christian king, the Negus (An-Najashi).

title: Conversion of Umar ibn Al-Khattab
description: Umar ibn Al-Khattab accepts Islam after hearing Quran recited at his sister Fatimah's home. His conversion strengthens the Muslim community significantly.

title: The Hijrah to Madinah
description: Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and the Muslims migrate from Makkah to Madinah, establishing the first Muslim community-state. This event marks the beginning of the Islamic calendar.

title: Constitution of Madinah
description: Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) establishes a charter governing relations between the Muslim and non-Muslim communities of Madinah, often regarded as one of the earliest constitutional documents.

title: Battle of Badr
description: The first major military engagement between the Muslims and the Quraysh. Despite being outnumbered three to one, the Muslim force of 313 achieves a decisive victory.

title: Battle of Uhud
description: The second major battle against the Quraysh. Initial Muslim success is reversed when archers leave their positions. Seventy Muslims are martyred, and Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) is wounded.

title: Battle of the Trench
description: A coalition of 10,000 besieges Madinah. The Muslims defend the city with an innovative trench strategy. The siege fails, marking the last major Quraysh offensive.

title: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
description: A peace treaty is signed between the Muslims and the Quraysh. Though initially seen as unfavorable, the Quran calls it a 'clear victory' (Surah al-Fath, 48:1). The truce allows Islam to spread rapidly.

title: Battle of Khaybar
description: The Muslim force besieges and captures the fortified oasis of Khaybar. Ali ibn Abi Talib leads the decisive assault after receiving the Prophet's banner.

title: Conquest of Makkah
description: The Muslims enter Makkah peacefully with an army of 10,000. Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) declares a general amnesty. The Ka'bah is cleansed of idols, and most Makkans accept Islam.

title: Battle of Hunayn
description: Shortly after the conquest of Makkah, the Muslim army faces an ambush from the Hawazin and Thaqif tribes. After initial confusion, they rally and achieve victory.

title: Expedition of Tabuk
description: The last major campaign of Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). An army of 30,000 marches north toward the Byzantine frontier. No battle occurs, but Muslim authority is established in the region.

title: The Farewell Pilgrimage
description: Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) performs his only Hajj and delivers the Farewell Sermon, establishing fundamental principles of justice, equality, and human rights.

title: Death of Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him)
description: Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) passes away in Madinah at the age of 63. Abu Bakr addresses the grieving community: 'Whoever worshipped Muhammad, know that Muhammad has died. And whoever worships Allah, know that Allah is Ever-Living and shall never die.'

title: Caliphate of Abu Bakr As-Siddiq
description: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq is chosen as the first Caliph. He consolidates the Muslim community, addresses the Ridda (apostasy) wars, and initiates the compilation of the Quran.

title: Caliphate of Umar ibn Al-Khattab
description: Umar ibn Al-Khattab becomes the second Caliph. His decade-long caliphate sees vast expansion, administrative reforms, the establishment of the Hijri calendar, and the founding of new cities.

title: Caliphate of Uthman ibn Affan
description: Uthman ibn Affan is selected as the third Caliph. His caliphate is marked by the standardization of the Quran into a single codex and continued expansion of the Muslim state.

title: Caliphate of Ali ibn Abi Talib
description: Ali ibn Abi Talib becomes the fourth and final Rightly Guided Caliph. His caliphate is marked by internal challenges and his efforts to maintain justice and unity.
